texts.text: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.3.1.1 The  present  chapter  gives  the  basic  description  of  the ERTMS/ETCS  system proposed to achieve technical interoperability.
texts.text: 2.3.1.1 The  present  chapter  gives  the  basic  description  of  the ERTMS/ETCS  system proposed to achieve technical interoperability.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description
texts.text: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP
texts.text: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.4.1.1 Due to the nature of the required functions, the ERTMS/ETCS system will have to be partly on the trackside and partly on board the trains.
texts.text: 2.4.1.1 Due to the nature of the required functions, the ERTMS/ETCS system will have to be partly on the trackside and partly on board the trains.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.4.1.2 This defines two subsystems, the on-board subsystem and the trackside subsystem.
texts.text: 2.4.1.2 This defines two subsystems, the on-board subsystem and the trackside subsystem.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.4.1.3 The environment of ERTMS/ETCS system is composed of:
texts.text: 2.4.1.3 The environment of ERTMS/ETCS system is composed of:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) the train, which will then be considered in the train interface specification;
texts.text: the train, which will then be considered in the train interface specification;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) the driver, which will then be considered via the driver interface specification;
texts.text: the driver, which will then be considered via the driver interface specification;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c)  other onboard interfaces (see architecture drawing in 2.5.3),
texts.text: other onboard interfaces (see architecture drawing in 2.5.3),

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d) external  trackside  systems  (interlockings,  control  centres,  etc.),  for  which  no interoperability requirement will be established.
texts.text: external  trackside  systems  (interlockings,  control  centres,  etc.),  for  which  no interoperability requirement will be established.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description
texts.text: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP
texts.text: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.1 Depending of the application level (see further sections), the trackside subsystem can be composed of:
texts.text: 2.5.1.1 Depending of the application level (see further sections), the trackside subsystem can be composed of:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.2.1 The  balise  is  a  transmission  device  that  can  send  telegrams  to  the  on-board subsystem.
texts.text: 2.5.1.2.1 The  balise  is  a  transmission  device  that  can  send  telegrams  to  the  on-board subsystem.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.2.2 The balise  is  based  on  the  existing  Eurobalise  specifications.  These  documents  are included in the frame of the ERTMS/ETCS specifications.
texts.text: 2.5.1.2.2 The balise  is  based  on  the  existing  Eurobalise  specifications.  These  documents  are included in the frame of the ERTMS/ETCS specifications.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.2.3 The balises provides the up-link, i. e. the possibility to send messages from trackside to the on-board subsystem.
texts.text: 2.5.1.2.3 The balises provides the up-link, i. e. the possibility to send messages from trackside to the on-board subsystem.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.2.4 The balises can provide fixed messages or, when connected to a lineside electronic unit, messages that can be changed.
texts.text: 2.5.1.2.4 The balises can provide fixed messages or, when connected to a lineside electronic unit, messages that can be changed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.2.5 The balises will be organised in groups, each balise transmitting a telegram and the combination of all telegrams defining the message sent by the balise group.
texts.text: 2.5.1.2.5 The balises will be organised in groups, each balise transmitting a telegram and the combination of all telegrams defining the message sent by the balise group.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.3.1 The lineside electronic units are electronic devices, that generate telegrams to be sent by balises, on basis of information received from external trackside systems.
texts.text: 2.5.1.3.1 The lineside electronic units are electronic devices, that generate telegrams to be sent by balises, on basis of information received from external trackside systems.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.4.1 The GSM-R radio communication network is used for the bi-directional exchange of messages between on-board subsystems and RBC or radio infill units.
texts.text: 2.5.1.4.1 The GSM-R radio communication network is used for the bi-directional exchange of messages between on-board subsystems and RBC or radio infill units.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description
texts.text: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP
texts.text: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.5.1 The RBC is a computer-based system that elaborates messages to be sent to the train on  basis  of  information  received  from  external  trackside  systems  and  on  basis  of information exchanged with the on-board subsystems.
texts.text: 2.5.1.5.1 The RBC is a computer-based system that elaborates messages to be sent to the train on  basis  of  information  received  from  external  trackside  systems  and  on  basis  of information exchanged with the on-board subsystems.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.5.2 The main objective of these messages is to provide movement authorities to allow the safe movement of trains on the Railway infrastructure area under the responsibility of the RBC.
texts.text: 2.5.1.5.2 The main objective of these messages is to provide movement authorities to allow the safe movement of trains on the Railway infrastructure area under the responsibility of the RBC.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.5.3 The interoperability requirements for the RBC are mainly related to the data exchange between the RBC and the on-board subsystem.
texts.text: 2.5.1.5.3 The interoperability requirements for the RBC are mainly related to the data exchange between the RBC and the on-board subsystem.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.6.1 The Euroloop subsystem operates on Level 1 lines, providing signalling information in advance as regard to the next main signal in the train running direction.
texts.text: 2.5.1.6.1 The Euroloop subsystem operates on Level 1 lines, providing signalling information in advance as regard to the next main signal in the train running direction.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.6.2 The Euroloop subsystem is composed of an on-board functionality and by one or more trackside parts.
texts.text: 2.5.1.6.2 The Euroloop subsystem is composed of an on-board functionality and by one or more trackside parts.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.7.1 The  RADIO  INFILL  subsystem  operates  on  Level  1  lines,  providing  signalling information in advance as regard to the next main signal in the train running direction.
texts.text: 2.5.1.7.1 The  RADIO  INFILL  subsystem  operates  on  Level  1  lines,  providing  signalling information in advance as regard to the next main signal in the train running direction.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.7.2 The RADIO INFILL subsystem is composed of an on-board functionality and by one or more trackside parts (named RADIO INFILL Unit).
texts.text: 2.5.1.7.2 The RADIO INFILL subsystem is composed of an on-board functionality and by one or more trackside parts (named RADIO INFILL Unit).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.8.1 The role of the KMC is to manage the cryptographic keys, which are used to secure the EURORADIO communications between the ERTMS/ETCS entities (ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipments, RBCs and RIUs).
texts.text: 2.5.1.8.1 The role of the KMC is to manage the cryptographic keys, which are used to secure the EURORADIO communications between the ERTMS/ETCS entities (ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipments, RBCs and RIUs).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.1.9.1 The role  of  the  PKI  is  to  manage  and  distribute  digital  certificates,  so  as  to  allow  a secure on-line distribution of cryptographic keys between KMCs and from a KMC to the ERTMS/ETCS entities (ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipments, RBCs and RIUs).
texts.text: 2.5.1.9.1 The role  of  the  PKI  is  to  manage  and  distribute  digital  certificates,  so  as  to  allow  a secure on-line distribution of cryptographic keys between KMCs and from a KMC to the ERTMS/ETCS entities (ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipments, RBCs and RIUs).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.2.1 Depending of the application level (see further sections), the on-board subsystem can be composed of:
texts.text: 2.5.2.1 Depending of the application level (see further sections), the on-board subsystem can be composed of:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) the on-board part of the GSM-R radio system;
texts.text: the on-board part of the GSM-R radio system;

texts.orig: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description
texts.text: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP
texts.text: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.2.2.1 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment is a computer-based system that supervises the movement of the train to which it belongs, on basis of information exchanged with the trackside subsystem.
texts.text: 2.5.2.2.1 The ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment is a computer-based system that supervises the movement of the train to which it belongs, on basis of information exchanged with the trackside subsystem.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.2.2.2 The  interoperability  requirements  for  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  are related to the functionality and the data exchange between the trackside subsystems and  the  on-board  subsystem  and  to  the  functional  data  exchange  between  the  onboard subsystem and:
texts.text: 2.5.2.2.2 The  interoperability  requirements  for  the  ERTMS/ETCS  on-board  equipment  are related to the functionality and the data exchange between the trackside subsystems and  the  on-board  subsystem  and  to  the  functional  data  exchange  between  the  onboard subsystem and:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c)  the onboard part of the existing national train control system(s).
texts.text: the onboard part of the existing national train control system(s).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.2.3.1 The  GSM-R  on-board  radio  system  is  used  for  the  bi-directional  exchange  of messages between on-board subsystem and RBC or radio infill unit.
texts.text: 2.5.2.3.1 The  GSM-R  on-board  radio  system  is  used  for  the  bi-directional  exchange  of messages between on-board subsystem and RBC or radio infill unit.

texts.orig: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description
texts.text: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description

texts.text: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

texts.label: footnote
texts.orig: (*) Depending on its functionality and the desired configuration, the national system can be addressed either via an STM using the standard interface or via another national solution
texts.text: (*) Depending on its functionality and the desired configuration, the national system can be addressed either via an STM using the standard interface or via another national solution

texts.orig: Figure 1: ERTMS/ETCS system and its interfaces
texts.text: Figure 1: ERTMS/ETCS system and its interfaces

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.5.3.1 Note: the entities inside the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment box are shown only to highlight the scope of the interfaces that are specified in the TSI CCS annex A.
texts.text: 2.5.3.1 Note: the entities inside the ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment box are shown only to highlight the scope of the interfaces that are specified in the TSI CCS annex A.

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP
texts.text: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.1.1 The different ERTMS/ETCS application levels (short: levels) are a way to express the possible operating relationships between track and train. Level definitions are related to  the  trackside  equipment  used,  to  the  way  trackside  information  reaches  the  onboard units and to which functions are processed in the trackside and in the on-board equipment respectively.
texts.text: 2.6.1.1 The different ERTMS/ETCS application levels (short: levels) are a way to express the possible operating relationships between track and train. Level definitions are related to  the  trackside  equipment  used,  to  the  way  trackside  information  reaches  the  onboard units and to which functions are processed in the trackside and in the on-board equipment respectively.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.1.2 Different  levels  have  been  defined  to  allow  each  individual  railway  administration  to select the appropriate ERTMS/ETCS  application trackside, according to their strategies, to their trackside infrastructure and to the required performance. Furthermore,  the different application levels permit the interfacing of individual signalling systems and train control systems to ERTMS/ETCS.
texts.text: 2.6.1.2 Different  levels  have  been  defined  to  allow  each  individual  railway  administration  to select the appropriate ERTMS/ETCS  application trackside, according to their strategies, to their trackside infrastructure and to the required performance. Furthermore,  the different application levels permit the interfacing of individual signalling systems and train control systems to ERTMS/ETCS.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.1.3 For the purpose of a consistent specification a level 0 has been defined. This level is used  for  operation  on  non-equipped  (unfitted)  lines  or  on  lines  equipped  with  train control system(s) but operation under their supervision is currently not possible.
texts.text: 2.6.1.3 For the purpose of a consistent specification a level 0 has been defined. This level is used  for  operation  on  non-equipped  (unfitted)  lines  or  on  lines  equipped  with  train control system(s) but operation under their supervision is currently not possible.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.2.1 A train equipped with ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment always co-operates with the ERTMS/ETCS trackside equipment in a defined ERTMS/ETCS level.
texts.text: 2.6.2.1 A train equipped with ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment always co-operates with the ERTMS/ETCS trackside equipment in a defined ERTMS/ETCS level.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.2.2 All transitions between levels are performed according to well-specified rules.
texts.text: 2.6.2.2 All transitions between levels are performed according to well-specified rules.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.2.3 ERTMS/ETCS can be configured to operate in one of the following application levels:
texts.text: 2.6.2.3 ERTMS/ETCS can be configured to operate in one of the following application levels:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  ERTMS/ETCS Level 0 (train equipped with ERTMS/ETCS operating on a line not equipped with any train control system (ERTMS/ETCS or national system) or on a line  equipped  with  ERTMS/ETCS  and/or  national  system(s)  but  operation  under their supervision is currently not possible)
texts.text:  ERTMS/ETCS Level 0 (train equipped with ERTMS/ETCS operating on a line not equipped with any train control system (ERTMS/ETCS or national system) or on a line  equipped  with  ERTMS/ETCS  and/or  national  system(s)  but  operation  under their supervision is currently not possible)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  ERTMS/ETCS Level NTC (train equipped with ERTMS/ETCS operating on a line equipped with a national system)
texts.text:  ERTMS/ETCS Level NTC (train equipped with ERTMS/ETCS operating on a line equipped with a national system)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  ERTMS/ETCS Application Level 1 with or without infill transmission (train equipped with  ERTMS/ETCS  operating  on  a  line  equipped  with  Eurobalises  and  optionally Euroloop or Radio infill)
texts.text:  ERTMS/ETCS Application Level 1 with or without infill transmission (train equipped with  ERTMS/ETCS  operating  on  a  line  equipped  with  Eurobalises  and  optionally Euroloop or Radio infill)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  ERTMS/ETCS Application Level 2 (train equipped with ERTMS/ETCS operating on a  line  controlled  by  a  Radio  Block  Centre  and  equipped  with  Eurobalises  and Euroradio) with train position and train integrity proving performed by the trackside
texts.text:  ERTMS/ETCS Application Level 2 (train equipped with ERTMS/ETCS operating on a  line  controlled  by  a  Radio  Block  Centre  and  equipped  with  Eurobalises  and Euroradio) with train position and train integrity proving performed by the trackside

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  ERTMS/ETCS Application Level 3 (similar to level 2 but with train position and train integrity supervision based on information received from the train)
texts.text:  ERTMS/ETCS Application Level 3 (similar to level 2 but with train position and train integrity supervision based on information received from the train)

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description
texts.text: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP
texts.text: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.2.4 It is possible to superimpose several application levels in parallel on the same track, for example  to  run  trains  without  train  integrity  device  in  level  2  and  in  parallel  trains equipped with train integrity device in level 3. Other examples might be a station which is shared by trains arriving over level 1 and level 2 lines (junctions) or parallel operation of a national system with ERTMS/ETCS. Mixed levels are supported.
texts.text: 2.6.2.4 It is possible to superimpose several application levels in parallel on the same track, for example  to  run  trains  without  train  integrity  device  in  level  2  and  in  parallel  trains equipped with train integrity device in level 3. Other examples might be a station which is shared by trains arriving over level 1 and level 2 lines (junctions) or parallel operation of a national system with ERTMS/ETCS. Mixed levels are supported.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.2.7 It  is  possible  to  transmit  information  not  intended  for  ERTMS/ETCS  but  for  other systems over the ERTMS/ETCS transmission channels. This information is not used by ERTMS/ETCS.
texts.text: 2.6.2.7 It  is  possible  to  transmit  information  not  intended  for  ERTMS/ETCS  but  for  other systems over the ERTMS/ETCS transmission channels. This information is not used by ERTMS/ETCS.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description
texts.text: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP
texts.text: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.3.1.1 Level 0 covers operation of ETCS equipped trains on lines not equipped with ETCS or national  systems  or  on  lines  where  trackside  ERTMS/ETCS  infrastructure  and/or national  systems  may  exist  but  operation  under  their  supervision  is  currently  not possible (e.g. commissioning or on-board/trackside failed components).
texts.text: 2.6.3.1.1 Level 0 covers operation of ETCS equipped trains on lines not equipped with ETCS or national  systems  or  on  lines  where  trackside  ERTMS/ETCS  infrastructure  and/or national  systems  may  exist  but  operation  under  their  supervision  is  currently  not possible (e.g. commissioning or on-board/trackside failed components).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.3.1.2 In  Level  0  it  is  authorized  to  operate  trains  without  any  train  control  system  and therefore  line  side  optical  signals  or  other  means  of  signalling  are  used  to  give movement authorities to the driver.
texts.text: 2.6.3.1.2 In  Level  0  it  is  authorized  to  operate  trains  without  any  train  control  system  and therefore  line  side  optical  signals  or  other  means  of  signalling  are  used  to  give movement authorities to the driver.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.3.1.3 ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment provides no supervision except of  the  maximum design speed of a train and maximum speed permitted in unfitted areas.
texts.text: 2.6.3.1.3 ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment provides no supervision except of  the  maximum design speed of a train and maximum speed permitted in unfitted areas.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.3.1.4 Train detection and  train integrity supervision are performed  by  the trackside equipment of the underlying signalling system (interlocking, track circuits etc.) and are outside the scope of ERTMS/ETCS.
texts.text: 2.6.3.1.4 Train detection and  train integrity supervision are performed  by  the trackside equipment of the underlying signalling system (interlocking, track circuits etc.) and are outside the scope of ERTMS/ETCS.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.3.1.5 Level  0  uses  no  track-train  transmission  except  Eurobalises  to  announce/command level  transitions.  Eurobalises  therefore  still  have  to  be  read.  No  balise  data  except certain special commands are interpreted.
texts.text: 2.6.3.1.5 Level  0  uses  no  track-train  transmission  except  Eurobalises  to  announce/command level  transitions.  Eurobalises  therefore  still  have  to  be  read.  No  balise  data  except certain special commands are interpreted.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.3.1.6 No supervisory information is indicated on the DMI except the train speed. Train data has  to  be  entered  in  order  not  to  have  to  stop  a  train  at  a  level  transition  to ERTMS/ETCS equipped area and to supervise maximum train speed.
texts.text: 2.6.3.1.6 No supervisory information is indicated on the DMI except the train speed. Train data has  to  be  entered  in  order  not  to  have  to  stop  a  train  at  a  level  transition  to ERTMS/ETCS equipped area and to supervise maximum train speed.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.6.3.2 Summary of characteristics of Application Level 0
texts.text: 2.6.3.2 Summary of characteristics of Application Level 0

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description
texts.text: System Requirements Specification Chapter 2 Basic System Description

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP
texts.text: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No ERTMS/ETCS trackside equipment is used except for Eurobalises to announce level transitions and other specific commands.
texts.text:  No ERTMS/ETCS trackside equipment is used except for Eurobalises to announce level transitions and other specific commands.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Supervision of maximum speed permitted in an unfitted area.
texts.text:  Supervision of maximum speed permitted in an unfitted area.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Reading of Eurobalises to detect level transitions and certain special commands. All other messages are rejected.
texts.text:  Reading of Eurobalises to detect level transitions and certain special commands. All other messages are rejected.

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP
texts.text: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.4.1.1 Level  NTC  is  used  to  run  ERTMS/ETCS  equipped  trains  on  lines  equipped  with national train control and speed supervision systems.
texts.text: 2.6.4.1.1 Level  NTC  is  used  to  run  ERTMS/ETCS  equipped  trains  on  lines  equipped  with national train control and speed supervision systems.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.4.1.2 Train  control  information  generated  trackside  by  the  national  train  control  system  is transmitted  to  the  train  via  the  communication  channels  of  the  underlying  national system.
texts.text: 2.6.4.1.2 Train  control  information  generated  trackside  by  the  national  train  control  system  is transmitted  to  the  train  via  the  communication  channels  of  the  underlying  national system.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.4.1.3 Note: Lineside optical signals might be necessary or not, depending on the performance and functionality of the underlying systems.
texts.text: 2.6.4.1.3 Note: Lineside optical signals might be necessary or not, depending on the performance and functionality of the underlying systems.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.4.1.5 The  achievable  level  of  supervision  is  similar  to  the  one  provided  by  the  underlying national systems.
texts.text: 2.6.4.1.5 The  achievable  level  of  supervision  is  similar  to  the  one  provided  by  the  underlying national systems.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.4.1.6 Train detection and train integrity supervision are performed by equipment external to ERTMS/ETCS.
texts.text: 2.6.4.1.6 Train detection and train integrity supervision are performed by equipment external to ERTMS/ETCS.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.4.1.7 Level NTC uses no ERTMS/ETCS track-train information except to announce/command  level transitions and specific commands  related to balise transmission. Eurobalises therefore still have to be read.
texts.text: 2.6.4.1.7 Level NTC uses no ERTMS/ETCS track-train information except to announce/command  level transitions and specific commands  related to balise transmission. Eurobalises therefore still have to be read.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.4.1.8 The information displayed to the driver depends on the functionality of the underlying national  system.  The  active  national  system  is  indicated  to  the  driver  as  part  of  that information. Full train data has to be entered in order not to have to stop a train at a level transition position and to supervise maximum train speed.
texts.text: 2.6.4.1.8 The information displayed to the driver depends on the functionality of the underlying national  system.  The  active  national  system  is  indicated  to  the  driver  as  part  of  that information. Full train data has to be entered in order not to have to stop a train at a level transition position and to supervise maximum train speed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.4.1.9 A combination of national systems can be regarded as one NTC level.
texts.text: 2.6.4.1.9 A combination of national systems can be regarded as one NTC level.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.4.1.10  Depending  on  the  functionality  and  the  configuration  of  the  specific  national  system installed onboard, the ERTMS/ETCS Onboard system may need to be interfaced to it, in order to perform the transitions from/to the national system and/or in order to give access to ERTMS/ETCS Onboard resources (e.g. DMI). This can be achieved through a device called an STM (Specific Transmission Module) using a standardised interface.
texts.text: 2.6.4.1.10  Depending  on  the  functionality  and  the  configuration  of  the  specific  national  system installed onboard, the ERTMS/ETCS Onboard system may need to be interfaced to it, in order to perform the transitions from/to the national system and/or in order to give access to ERTMS/ETCS Onboard resources (e.g. DMI). This can be achieved through a device called an STM (Specific Transmission Module) using a standardised interface.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.6.4.2 Summary of characteristics of Application Level NTC
texts.text: 2.6.4.2 Summary of characteristics of Application Level NTC

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Level  NTC  uses  the  track-train  transmission  system  from  an  underlying  national system, which is not part of ERTMS/ETCS.
texts.text:  Level  NTC  uses  the  track-train  transmission  system  from  an  underlying  national system, which is not part of ERTMS/ETCS.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  For level transition purposes Eurobalises are used.
texts.text:  For level transition purposes Eurobalises are used.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Onboard part of the national system.
texts.text:  Onboard part of the national system.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No train supervision, it is fully handed over to the national system.
texts.text:  No train supervision, it is fully handed over to the national system.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Reading of Eurobalises to detect level transitions and certain special commands. All other messages are rejected.
texts.text:  Reading of Eurobalises to detect level transitions and certain special commands. All other messages are rejected.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Management of the national system through STM, in case the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment is interfaced to the national system through an STM.
texts.text:  Management of the national system through STM, in case the ERTMS/ETCS onboard equipment is interfaced to the national system through an STM.

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP
texts.text: ERA * UNISIG * EEIG ERTMS USERS GROUP

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.5.1.1 ERTMS/ETCS Level 1 is a spot transmission based train control system to be used as an overlay on an underlying signalling system.
texts.text: 2.6.5.1.1 ERTMS/ETCS Level 1 is a spot transmission based train control system to be used as an overlay on an underlying signalling system.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.5.1.2 Movement  authorities  are  generated  trackside  and  are  transmitted  to  the  train  via Eurobalises.
texts.text: 2.6.5.1.2 Movement  authorities  are  generated  trackside  and  are  transmitted  to  the  train  via Eurobalises.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.5.1.3 ERTMS/ETCS Level 1 provides a continuous speed supervision  system,  which  also protects against overrun of the authority.
texts.text: 2.6.5.1.3 ERTMS/ETCS Level 1 provides a continuous speed supervision  system,  which  also protects against overrun of the authority.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.5.1.4 Train detection and  train integrity supervision are performed  by  the trackside equipment of the underlying signalling system (interlocking, track circuits etc.) and are outside the scope of ERTMS/ETCS.
texts.text: 2.6.5.1.4 Train detection and  train integrity supervision are performed  by  the trackside equipment of the underlying signalling system (interlocking, track circuits etc.) and are outside the scope of ERTMS/ETCS.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.5.1.5 Level 1 is based on Eurobalises as spot transmission devices.
texts.text: 2.6.5.1.5 Level 1 is based on Eurobalises as spot transmission devices.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.5.1.6 The trackside equipment does not know the train to which it sends information.
texts.text: 2.6.5.1.6 The trackside equipment does not know the train to which it sends information.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.5.1.7 If  in  level  1  a  lineside  signal  clears,  an  approaching  train  can  not  receive  this information until it passes the Eurobalise group at that signal. The driver therefore has to  observe  the  lineside  signal  to  know  when  to  proceed.  The  train  has  then  to  be permitted  to  approach  the  stopping  location  below  a  maximum  permitted  release speed.
texts.text: 2.6.5.1.7 If  in  level  1  a  lineside  signal  clears,  an  approaching  train  can  not  receive  this information until it passes the Eurobalise group at that signal. The driver therefore has to  observe  the  lineside  signal  to  know  when  to  proceed.  The  train  has  then  to  be permitted  to  approach  the  stopping  location  below  a  maximum  permitted  release speed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.5.1.8 Additional Eurobalises can be placed between distant and main signals to transmit infill information, the train will receive new information before reaching the signal.
texts.text: 2.6.5.1.8 Additional Eurobalises can be placed between distant and main signals to transmit infill information, the train will receive new information before reaching the signal.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.5.1.9 Note: Lineside signals are required in level 1 applications, except if semi-continuous infill is provided.
texts.text: 2.6.5.1.9 Note: Lineside signals are required in level 1 applications, except if semi-continuous infill is provided.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.5.1.10  Semi-continuous infill can be provided using Euroloop or radio infill. In this case, the on-board system will  be  able  to  show  new  information  to  the  driver  as  soon  as  it  is available and even at standstill.
texts.text: 2.6.5.1.10  Semi-continuous infill can be provided using Euroloop or radio infill. In this case, the on-board system will  be  able  to  show  new  information  to  the  driver  as  soon  as  it  is available and even at standstill.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.5.1.11  Euroloop or radio infill  can  improve  the  safety  of  a  level  1  system  as  they  allow  the operation without release speed.
texts.text: 2.6.5.1.11  Euroloop or radio infill  can  improve  the  safety  of  a  level  1  system  as  they  allow  the operation without release speed.

texts.text:  Eurobalises for spot transmission from track to train.

texts.text:  Eurobalises must be able to transmit variable information.

texts.label: list_item

texts.text:  Semi continuous infill transmission by using Euroloop or radio infill is optional.

texts.text:  Determine movement authorities according to the underlying signalling system.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Transmit movement authorities and track description data to the train.
texts.text:  Transmit movement authorities and track description data to the train.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Euroloop transmission if infill by Euroloop is required.
texts.text:  Euroloop transmission if infill by Euroloop is required.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Radio infill transmission if infill by radio is required.
texts.text:  Radio infill transmission if infill by radio is required.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Reception of  movement authority and track description related to the transmitting balise.
texts.text:  Reception of  movement authority and track description related to the transmitting balise.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Selection  of  the  most  restrictive  value  of  the  different  speeds  permitted  at  each location ahead.
texts.text:  Selection  of  the  most  restrictive  value  of  the  different  speeds  permitted  at  each location ahead.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Calculation of a dynamic speed profile taking into account the train running/braking characteristics which are known on-board and the track description data.
texts.text:  Calculation of a dynamic speed profile taking into account the train running/braking characteristics which are known on-board and the track description data.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Comparison of  the  train  speed  with  the  permitted  speed  and  commanding  of  the brake application if necessary.

texts.orig: 2.6.6.1.1 ERTMS/ETCS  Level  2  is  a  radio  based  train  control  system  which  is  used  as  an overlay on an underlying signalling system.
texts.text: 2.6.6.1.1 ERTMS/ETCS  Level  2  is  a  radio  based  train  control  system  which  is  used  as  an overlay on an underlying signalling system.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.6.1.2 Movement  authorities  are  generated  trackside  and  are  transmitted  to  the  train  via Euroradio.
texts.text: 2.6.6.1.2 Movement  authorities  are  generated  trackside  and  are  transmitted  to  the  train  via Euroradio.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.6.1.3 ERTMS/ETCS Level 2 provides a continuous speed supervision  system,  which  also protects against overrun of the authority.
texts.text: 2.6.6.1.3 ERTMS/ETCS Level 2 provides a continuous speed supervision  system,  which  also protects against overrun of the authority.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.6.1.4 Train detection and  train integrity supervision are performed  by  the trackside equipment of the underlying signalling system (interlocking, track circuits etc.) and are outside the scope of ERTMS/ETCS.
texts.text: 2.6.6.1.4 Train detection and  train integrity supervision are performed  by  the trackside equipment of the underlying signalling system (interlocking, track circuits etc.) and are outside the scope of ERTMS/ETCS.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.6.1.5 Level 2 is based on Euroradio for track to train communication and on Eurobalises as spot transmission devices mainly for location referencing.
texts.text: 2.6.6.1.5 Level 2 is based on Euroradio for track to train communication and on Eurobalises as spot transmission devices mainly for location referencing.

texts.label: list_item

texts.text: 2.6.6.1.6 The  trackside  radio  block  centre  which  provides  the  information  to  the  trains  knows each  ERTMS/ETCS  controlled  train  individually  by  the  ERTMS/ETCS  identity  of  its leading ERTMS/ETCS on-board equipment.

texts.text: 2.6.6.1.7 Note: Lineside signals can be suppressed in Level 2.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.8.1 An ERTMS/ETCS equipment which is not isolated always operates in one of the above described  levels.  All  transitions  between  these  levels  are  performed  according  to defined functions and procedures.
texts.text: 2.6.8.1 An ERTMS/ETCS equipment which is not isolated always operates in one of the above described  levels.  All  transitions  between  these  levels  are  performed  according  to defined functions and procedures.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.8.2 Additional national functions and rules which might be used by an individual railway to for  example  prevent  not  equipped  trains  from  entering  a  level  2/3  area  are  not specified here and have to be implemented outside ERTMS/ETCS.
texts.text: 2.6.8.2 Additional national functions and rules which might be used by an individual railway to for  example  prevent  not  equipped  trains  from  entering  a  level  2/3  area  are  not specified here and have to be implemented outside ERTMS/ETCS.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2.6.8.3 The following table shows all possible transitions (marked with Grey):
texts.text: 2.6.8.3 The following table shows all possible transitions (marked with Grey):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Transitions  between  level  NTC  and  level  NTC  describe  the  switching  from  one national system to another national system.
texts.text: Transitions  between  level  NTC  and  level  NTC  describe  the  switching  from  one national system to another national system.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Transitions  between  level  2  and  level  2  respectively  between  level  3  and  level  3 describe the handover between RBC's.
texts.text: Transitions  between  level  2  and  level  2  respectively  between  level  3  and  level  3 describe the handover between RBC's.
